Y.value: The number of trials required to obtain a successful experiment (geometric random variable with parameter p)
Y.description: The number of trials required to obtain a successful result. Follows a geometric distribution with success probability p. Used to compute the expected cost via C(Y) = 25000Y + 5000(Y-1).

cost_per_trial.description: The cost of performing each experiment trial, $25,000, used in the cost function C(Y).

setup_cost_per_failure.description: The cost to set up for the next trial after a failure, $5,000, used in the cost function C(Y).
